sound_type: moaning + teeth-chattering -- eerie mating moans 300-2,000 Hz, warning teeth-chatter. E=3
ecosystem: Mixed forest. Tree canopy and ground. Coniferous and deciduous forests. Nocturnal.
season: Year-round resident. Mating moans peak September-November. Nocturnal.

coordinates.description: Porcupine in PNW mixed forests. Nocturnal tree-climber. Year-round resident in coniferous and deciduous forests.

taxonomy_note.description: FIRST Erethizontidae. New World porcupine family. Only porcupine in North America. Second consecutive new rodent family (206 Aplodontidae).
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Erethizontidae: North American Porcupine (207, E=3). Sole entry. Only N.A. porcupine.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Erethizon from Greek 'erethizo' (to irritate). dorsatum from Latin 'dorsum' (back) -- referring to quilled back.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Erethizon -- monotypic genus. E. dorsatum is the only species.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Rodentia. Second consecutive new rodent family (Aplodontidae 206, Erethizontidae 207).
taxonomy_note.family_position: Erethizontidae: FIRST entry. New World porcupines. Convergent with Old World Hystricidae.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. Third-largest N.A. rodent (after beaver and nutria). 30,000 quills. Nocturnal arboreal.

song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Moans: 300-2,000 Hz. Teeth-chatter: 500-5,000 Hz. Whines: varied.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Moans: 2-8 sec. Teeth-chatter: continuous while threatened.
song_metrics.note_structure: Long wavering moans (mating). Rapid staccato clicks (warning). Soft grunts (social).
song_metrics.note_types: Mating moan (eerie, human-like), teeth-chattering (warning), whine, squeal, grunt.
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Moans: repeated at intervals during calling bouts. Teeth-chatter: continuous.
song_metrics.timbre: Moans: eerie, wavering, almost human. Teeth-chatter: sharp, staccato clicking. Both unsettling.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=3. Moderate volume. Maximum unsettlement. Quality over volume. Night sounds.
song_metrics.tempo: Moans: slow, 2-8 sec each. Teeth-chatter: rapid, continuous.
song_metrics.context: Moans: fall mating (Sep-Nov), males calling. Teeth-chatter: close-range warning, quills raised.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal across range. Consistent vocalization patterns.
song_metrics.key_property: MOANING + TEETH-CHATTERING. E=3. FIRST ERETHIZONTIDAE. 30,000 quills. Nocturnal. Eerie mating moans. Warning chatter. v1.49.348.

pelage.overall: Dark brown to black guard hairs over yellow-white quills. 5-14 kg. Stocky. Short legs. Heavy tail.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Slow-moving, stocky rodent with visible quills. Often seen in trees (look for stripped bark). Waddles when walking.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Winter: thicker guard hairs over quills. Summer: thinner coat. Quills year-round.

physical_description.overall: 5-14 kg (12-30 lbs). 60-90 cm body + 15-30 cm tail. ~30,000 quills. Stocky. Curved claws for climbing.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Slow, dark, stocky animal in tree or waddling on ground. Quills visible when raised. Stripped bark in conifers = feeding sign.
physical_description.field_id: Look for stripped bark patches high in conifers (winter feeding). At night, listen for eerie moaning in fall. Slow-moving dark shape on trails at dusk/dawn. Do not approach -- quills embed on contact.

nodes.title: First Erethizontidae -- New World Porcupines
nodes.description: New family. Convergent quill evolution with Old World Hystricidae. Arboreal. Only N.A. porcupine. Second consecutive new rodent family.

nodes.title: 30,000 Quills -- Deterrence Biology
nodes.description: Barbed tips, antibiotic coating, contact-release. Not projectiles. Fisher is sole effective predator. Speed unnecessary.

nodes.title: Eerie Mating Moans -- Quality Over Volume
nodes.description: Human-sounding moans at 300-2,000 Hz. Mistaken for Bigfoot. E=3 by volume, maximum unsettlement by quality.

nodes.title: Arboreal Paradox -- Climber Who Falls
nodes.description: 30-40% show healed fractures. Antibiotic quill coating protects against self-quilling. Falls are occupational hazard.

cross_references.relationship: Two consecutive new rodent families! Aplodontidae (206) then Erethizontidae (207). Both nocturnal. E=2 whisper vs. E=3 moan.
Impact vs. deterrence. Ram seeks contact (E=6). Porcupine warns against it (E=3). Opposite strategies.
Bear warning (E=5, huff) vs. Porcupine warning (E=3, teeth-chatter). Both escalation signals. Different volume.
DETERRENCE! Lo-fi deters polish-seekers. Quills deter predators. Both unappealing to wrong audience.
